meta.description: Gregor Mendel — genetics mentor, patient experimenter, and gardener-monk. Trigger this skill when users ask about genetics, heredity, inheritance patterns, Mendelian laws, dominant/recessive traits, gene segregation, independent assortment, Punnett squares, classical genetics, or

functional_suitability.note: All promised domains covered; evidence signaling (three-tier) is internally consistent with no self-contradiction

reliability.note: Safety-framework covers eugenics, personal health, crisis; no explicit handling for clearly off-topic requests

performance_context.note: All 4 reference files have explicit conditional trigger conditions; SKILL.md ~167 lines

agent_usability.note: Excellent learnability, consistency, and error prevention; Feedback Design loses one point — Punnett square output format undefined

human_usability.note: Broad natural trigger language; 3-level adaptive depth with explicit garden-default fallback

security.note: No credentials; safety-framework handles high-risk inputs; eugenics protocol explicit

maintainability.note: Clean 5-file modular structure; no module expansion documentation (unlike Hippocrates); evals.json present

agent_specific.note: Strong across all five criteria; composability with docx/pdf skills referenced but not deeply specified

key_strengths: Biographical grounding is unusually rich — humor, bee anecdotes, Fisher controversy, hawkweed failure all handled with precision and warmth
Eugenics addressed directly and in-persona rather than deflected — strongest adversarial handling in the skill hub so far
Rhythm pattern (slow walk → moment of recognition) is well-differentiated from Hippocrates — sensory and garden-grounded rather than philosophical
Evidence signaling system (three-tier: classical / extended / post-Mendel) is internally consistent with no self-contradiction
Safety-framework covers a sensitive domain (genetics + eugenics history) with clear protocols that preserve the teaching persona

recommendations.title: No module expansion documentation
recommendations.problem: No guidance on how to add specialty modules (epigenetics, population genetics, modern genomics). Hippocrates has an explicit Module Expansion section; Mendel does not, making extension harder to do consistently.
recommendations.root_cause: Module expansion section was not carried over from the Hippocrates template when Mendel was designed.
recommendations.fix: Add a Module Expansion section to SKILL.md documenting the planned file structure for future reference modules (e.g., references/modern-genetics.md, references/population-genetics.md) and when each would be triggered.

recommendations.title: Punnett square output format underspecified
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md says 'offer to draw it out or create an interactive diagram' but provides no concrete format. Output consistency for Punnett squares is not guaranteed across runs or models.
recommendations.root_cause: Visual teaching format was mentioned as a capability without specifying a preferred rendering approach.
recommendations.fix: Add a short format example to genetics-guide.md showing the preferred Punnett square rendering (ASCII table or markdown grid), so agents produce consistent output without guessing.

recommendations.title: Composability with docx/pdf skills lightly documented
recommendations.problem: Integration with docx and pdf output skills is referenced in Output Formats but not specified. An agent invoking those skills would not know what input format to pass.
recommendations.root_cause: Same gap present in Hippocrates — integration points referenced but not specified.
recommendations.fix: Add one line to Output Formats: 'Pass the full response text to the docx or pdf skill; no special formatting required prior to handoff.'
